name: Forced Liquidation
tagline: Notion Thief + Psychosis Crawler + any wheel -> one-wheel table kill (statics fire through Grand Abolisher)
idea: This deck weaponizes drawing cards as a damage source.

briefKills.line: Notion Thief + Psychosis Crawler + any wheel (Niv-Mizzet, Parun = redundant version) → you draw ~28 (your 7 + ~21 redirected) → each opponent loses ~28 AND draws nothing

2+ opponent-draw punishers (Underworld Dreams / Fate Unraveler / Sheoldred / Ob Nixilis) + a wheel · Bloodletter of Aclazotz doubles it → each opponent draws 7 → ~14–28 life loss each (lethal-or-bust — never spin on one)

openings: Weakest axis — Durability (3/5): the judges' soft spot.
Lethal-or-bust: keep needs 2 key pieces / a tutor / board development — never a naked wheel.
Decap never-closes in 8% of goldfish games — a grind risk.
Tables (all three) never-close 18% of the time.

clock.src: lab kfk_clock_lab @40k · lethal-or-bust >=2-punisher gate + Kefka summoning-sickness fix 2026-07-02 (was T8/T9; +1 turn)
clock.caption: Decap (one opponent) lands around T9; the full table around T10.

gamePlan: This deck weaponizes *drawing cards* as a damage source. The shell is ~7 wheel effects (Windfall, Echo of Eons, Reforge the Soul, Magus of the Wheel, Jace's Archivist, Molten Psyche, Dark Deal) plus Peer into the Abyss, backed by a redundant suite of static punishers that convert every draw or discard into life loss. Because the kill is a *wheel resolved on your own turn* feeding *static* triggers — not casts or activated abilities — it fires straight through a Grand Abolisher lock, which is the entire reason this deck exists (it answers the pod's recurring T6–7 Abolisher combo archenemy that the counter-heavy decks can't). Kefka himself is the value/disruption engine and a deliberate removal-magnet, **not** a combo linchpin: his ETB (each player discards, you draw one per card *type* discarded) is guaranteed value per cast, and the win runs entirely from the command zone if he dies.
winLine: Notion Thief + Psychosis Crawler + any wheel -> one-wheel table kill (statics fire through Grand Abolisher)

finishers.name: Notion Thief + Psychosis Crawler + any wheel
finishers.note: Notion Thief redirects every opponent's wheel-draws to you; Psychosis Crawler pings each opponent 1 per *your* draw.

finishers.name: Multi-punisher wheel
finishers.note: with 2+ opponent-draw punishers down (Underworld Dreams, Fate Unraveler, Kederekt Parasite, Ob Nixilis the Hate-Twisted, Sheoldred), one wheel makes each opponent draw 7 → ~14–28 damage/life-loss each.

finishers.name: Peer into the Abyss
finishers.note: pointed at one opponent with a draw-punisher out: they draw ~half their library (→ that much damage) and lose half their life.

finishers.name: Displacer Kitten + Aether Channeler + Sol Ring + Mana Vault
finishers.note: CSB-registered complete combo `1170-1393-2364-5034`: infinite ETB + infinite colorless mana; route Channeler's "draw a card" ETB each iteration into Niv-Mizzet (1 dmg/draw) or Psychosis Crawler (each opp −1/draw) for a lethal that's commander-independent and resolves on your turn.

rulings.name: Notion Thief is a replacement, not a trigger
rulings.note: it uses no stack and can't be countered. It redirects to you every opponent draw except the first one in each of their draw steps; wheel draws aren't draw-step draws, so all of them are stolen. Flash it in before the wheel resolves.

rulings.name: Notion Thief turns OFF your opponent-draw punishers
rulings.note: while it's out, opponents don't draw (you do), so Underworld Dreams / Fate Unraveler won't fire on a wheel. Psychosis Crawler carries that kill instead. Don't plan on both at once.

rulings.name: Psychosis Crawler counts YOUR draws
rulings.note: each opponent loses 1 life per card you draw, once per card (its ruling: multiple draws → multiple triggers). Notion Thief + a 7-card wheel = ~7 per opponent stolen onto you = a ~21 table swing. The marquee line.

rulings.name: Kefka draws per card TYPE, not per card
rulings.note: each player discards one card; you draw one card for each distinct type among them (three lands → 1 draw; land + instant + creature → 3). And those are your draws, so they ping through Psychosis Crawler.

rulings.name: Peer into the Abyss rounds UP both halves
rulings.note: aimed at an opponent with the draw-punishers out, they ping themselves drawing half their library; aimed at an opponent under your Notion Thief, you draw their half-library (Crawler hammers the table) and they still lose half their life. It only refuels that one player, never the table.

rulings.name: Underworld Dreams / Fate Unraveler ping per card and only on OPPONENTS' draws
rulings.note: a "put into hand" that doesn't use the word draw (a tutor) doesn't ping; a wheel or Peer does.

rulings.name: The wheel-kill resolves THROUGH a Grand Abolisher
rulings.note: it's a sorcery cast on your turn feeding static triggers, with no targeting of opponents' permanents and no stack interaction with their combo, so their Abolisher (which only locks you out on their turn) can't stop it. The entire reason this deck exists.

rulings.name: Displacer Kitten triggers on NONCREATURE spells only
rulings.note: the backup combo (Kitten + Aether Channeler + Sol Ring + Mana Vault) blinks a rock for net mana and Channeler for a draw each cast. Don't blink a token (it vanishes), and remember the blink strips counters and auras.

keep.mixed: lethal-or-bust: keep needs 2 key pieces / a tutor / board development — never a naked wheel

mull.strategy: Lethal-or-bust discipline starts in the opener: never keep a hand whose only action is a wheel — a wheel without punishers refuels the whole pod. Keep 2–4 lands (rocks count double — Kefka is a 5-drop) with two punishers, or a punisher plus a tutor, or a tutor plus mana development. A lone Notion Thief also waits — it does nothing without a wheel and switches off your opponent-draw punishers. The sim's keep model was re-tuned to this rule on 2026-07-03 (needs 2 key pieces, a tutor, or board development — a naked wheel no longer keeps), so the dashboard drill's verdicts now match this guidance.

killTree.title: Forced Liquidation — Kefka, Court Mage
killTree.root:
Grixis 'forced draw' punisher shell — static draw/discard punishers in play
the kill is a WHEEL cast on YOUR turn feeding STATICS, so it fires THROUGH Grand Abolisher
killTree.stall:
assemble + protect ~2 punishers to the wheel turn (lethal-or-bust — a half-loaded wheel refuels the pod);
kill Grand Abolisher / draw-deniers on sight, and don't stack Notion Thief with the opponent-draw punishers
killTree.caption: cheapest line first · fall to the next

lines.need:
Notion Thief + Psychosis Crawler + any wheel
(Niv-Mizzet, Parun = redundant version)
lines.kill: you draw ~28 (your 7 + ~21 redirected) → each opponent loses ~28 AND draws nothing

lines.need:
2+ opponent-draw punishers (Underworld Dreams / Fate Unraveler / Sheoldred / Ob Nixilis)
+ a wheel · Bloodletter of Aclazotz doubles it
lines.kill: each opponent draws 7 → ~14–28 life loss each (lethal-or-bust — never spin on one)

lines.need:
Peer into the Abyss aimed at one opponent
with a draw-punisher out
lines.kill: they draw ~half their library → that much damage + lose half their life

lines.need:
Displacer Kitten + Aether Channeler + Sol Ring + Mana Vault
+ Niv-Mizzet or Psychosis Crawler (all in play)
lines.kill: infinite ETB → infinite draw → lethal (Abolisher-proof, commander-independent)

axisGloss.FINDING: keep a key piece / tutor / 2+ selection cards
axisGloss.BOARD: deploy the commander ~on curve + have an early (cmc≤3) play
